description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the jhipster/jhipster:v5.6.0 Docker image, defining stages build, test, analyze, package, release, and deploy, and a cache keyed by the branch that stores the .gradle/ directory. Add a before_script that sets GRADLE_USER_HOME to the repository .gradle folder and runs ./gradlew npm_install -PnodeInstall --no-daemon for all jobs. In the test stage add a frontend-test job that runs ./gradlew npm_test -PnodeInstall --no-daemon and publishes the build/test-results/ and build/jacoco/ directories as artifacts with JUnit reports from build/test-results/jest/TESTS-*.xml, and a gradle-test job that runs ./gradlew test -PnodeInstall --no-daemon and publishes similar test result artifacts. In the build stage add a gradle-compile job that runs ./gradlew compileJava -x check -PnodeInstall --no-daemon and stores the compiled classes and generated sources as artifacts. In the package stage add a gradle-package job that runs ./gradlew bootWar -Pprod -x check --no-daemon and saves the generated WAR files as artifacts. Finally, in the analyze stage add a sonar-analyze job that depends on gradle-test and frontend-test, runs ./gradlew sonarqube --no-daemon with SonarCloud parameters and a SONAR_TOKEN variable, allows failure, and does not produce artifacts.
